Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)

Fix pip install for Ubuntu 24.04 PEP 668 bare-mode startup

Ubuntu 24.04 uses externally-managed Python (PEP 668). Running plain
pip3 install fails with an error that was silently swallowed by || true.
Now tries --user first (safe, no sudo), then --break-system-packages
as fallback. This fixes NeilWin WSL2 bare-mode paho-mqtt installation.

Fix dashboard weekly CC: add 7+14 DTE targets; sync prices

Two fixes:

1. Weekly opportunities missing from dashboard
   fetch_option_chain_sync short_targets was [21,30,45,60] — 7 and 14
   DTE were never targeted so weekly option chains were never fetched,
   making it impossible for any weekly CC to be generated.
   Fix: short_targets = [7, 14, 21, 30, 45, 60]

2. Dashboard stock prices out of sync with CC screener
   Dashboard endpoint (/screener/dashboard-opportunities) was returning
   frozen EOD prices. Applied same market_snapshot_cache overlay as
   precomputed_scans.py so dashboard prices match the CC screener.

After next EOD run, dashboard will show Top 5 Weekly + Top 5 Monthly.

commit.message:
fix: admin endpoints crash with AuthResult unpacking error (#491)

## Summary

- Five admin endpoints in `users.py` crashed with `ValueError: too many
values to unpack (expected 3)` because `require_admin` now returns
`AuthResult` (4-value `__iter__`) but these endpoints still used 3-tuple
unpacking
- Fixed by using `AuthResult` properties directly
(`auth_data.user_email`) instead of tuple unpacking
- Audit trail improved: now logs actual admin email instead of truncated
token prefix

**Affected endpoints:** `POST /admin/credits`, `POST
/admin/users/{email}/disable`, `POST /admin/users/{email}/enable`,
`DELETE /admin/users/{email}`, `POST /admin/users/{email}/role`

## Test plan

- [x] 10 new unit tests covering all 5 fixed endpoints (success,
failure, unicode regression)
- [x] All 1118 existing tests still pass
- [x] Reproduced 500 error via curl before fix, confirmed fix resolves
it

@coderabbitai ignore

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Optimize newsletter quality: fix filtering pipeline and enhance writing skills

- Twitter: RT dedup, AI relevance filter, engagement tiebreaker, per-account cap (3), raise cap 15→30
- GitHub: evergreen repo blocklist (tensorflow, pytorch, etc.), only blocks trending not releases
- Agent filter: select 18-22 (was 25-30), hard filters for sentiment/political/stale content, GitHub MAX 2
- RSS: skip items older than 7 days via pubDate check
- Validation: require MODEL LITERACY + PICK OF THE DAY sections, forbidden phrase detection (EN+ZH)
- EN/ZH SKILL.md: rename sections (MODEL/DEV/TECHNIQUE/PRODUCT), add MODEL LITERACY + PICK OF THE DAY,
  safety rules, forbidden phrases, editorial voice, engagement display mandatory, target 15-22 items
- Stage 4/5 prompts: enforce new sections, engagement display, safety rules
- formatEngagement: "engagement" → "likes" for clarity

commit.message:
fix: BookReader height accounts for global audio player (#464)

Layout sets --player-h CSS custom property (5rem when player visible,
0px otherwise) on <main>. BookReader references it via
calc(100vh - 8rem - var(--player-h, 0px)) so the reader shrinks to
avoid being overlapped by the fixed-position global player.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add caller audio effects for real-world condition simulation (#26)

Implement 8 DSP effects + 13 accent presets to simulate real-world audio conditions (noise, speed, speakerphone, mic distance, clarity, accents, packet loss, jitter). Conversation tests use exact values for reproducibility; load tests randomize per caller via pool config. Effects apply to all caller TTS audio before sending to agent, testing robustness across diverse audio conditions.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

T6: Implement chaotic systems data pipeline

Adds src/data/chaos.py with:
- Lorenz-63 (RK4), Mackey-Glass DDE (Euler with history buffer), KS PDE (Lawson-RK4)
- generate_trajectory(), split_trajectory(), normalize(), lyapunov_time(), max_lyapunov_exponent()
- Lorenz LE ≈ 0.906 verified; MG chaos threshold at τ≈17 verified; KS positive LE verified
- TrajectoryDataset for PyTorch DataLoader compatibility

commit.message:
Fix iOS safe area background: use DOM element with negative env() insets

iOS Safari only propagates background-color (not background-image) into
safe areas. A real fixed DOM element with negative env(safe-area-inset-*)
values physically extends past the safe area boundaries, carrying the wood
texture and vignette into the status bar and home indicator regions.

commit.message:
Unify ace run: tasks + workflows in one command (#4)

* Add `ace run` pattern runner command

Implements a Fabric-like pattern runner that executes pre-configured
system prompts via the local Python workflow engine (aceteam-nodes).
Supports stdin pipes, inline text, file/folder I/O, and batch processing.

- Extract ensurePython() to shared util for reuse across commands
- Add 10 built-in patterns (7 general + 3 government)
- Support user-defined patterns via ~/.ace/patterns/<name>/system.md
- Add comprehensive tests for patterns, I/O, and command structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix command injection and path traversal vulnerabilities

- Replace all execSync shell-string calls with execFileSync array form
  to prevent command injection via config-sourced python paths
- Validate pattern names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$/ to
  prevent path traversal in user pattern loading
- Update tests to match execFileSync API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add police-report-writer pattern for government category

Adds a pattern that generates structured incident reports from officer
notes with incident header, involved persons, narrative, and evidence
sections. Follows Canadian police report formatting conventions.

Ref: aceteam-ai/aceteam#1774

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add three-tier on-ramp, node validation, and interactive TUI

- Provider detection: auto-detect OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, AceTeam
- Enhanced init: tiered guidance (free/BYO key/platform), post-setup
  demo with live execution or canned output fallback
- Node cache: validate workflow node types against cached list-nodes
  with 24h TTL before running, clear errors for unknown types
- Interactive TUI: menu-driven mode via `ace` (no args + TTY) using
  @inquirer/prompts — run patterns, workflows, create from templates,
  manage config, connect to platform
- Fix police-report-writer pattern excluded from BUILTIN_PATTERNS array
- Usability audit fixes per Nielsen's heuristics: provider check before
  input collection, workflow input discovery, overwrite confirmation,
  classified error messages, bounded loop instead of recursion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Migrate to workflow engine v2 schema and add uv toolchain support

- Migrate all workflow generation (patternToWorkflow) and templates to v2
  schema: input_node/inner_nodes/output_node/edges replacing
  nodes/inputs/outputs/input_edges/output_edges
- Use correct field schema format ({"type": "string"} not {"value_type": "string"})
- Remove temperature/max_tokens from LLM node params (not in v2 schema)
- Add uv as preferred Python toolchain for venv creation and package install,
  with fallback to native python/pip
- Update workflow validate command to accept both v1 and v2 schema formats
- Update node-cache to extract types from inner_nodes (v2) or nodes (v1)
- Update workflow lifecycle tests for v2 schema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tests for v2 schema, uv toolchain, provider detection, node cache, demos

63 new tests covering:
- Provider detection priority (AceTeam > OpenAI > Anthropic > Ollama > null)
- Node type cache with TTL, Python fallback, corrupt cache handling
- uv toolchain: findUv, createVenv with/without uv, installAceteamNodes
- v2 workflow schema: patternToWorkflow output, field schemas, LLM params
- Template v2 compliance: no v1 fields, correct field types, edge validity
- Canned demo outputs for no-provider fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Unify ace run to handle both tasks and workflow JSON files

Users found "ace run" vs "ace workflow run" confusing. Now "ace run"
auto-detects .json arguments as workflow files, removing the need for
a separate subcommand. "ace workflow" retains authoring commands
(validate, create, list-nodes, list-templates). Also adds PR
description guidelines to CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Install aceteam-nodes[llm] and add Why/What/How PR convention

- Install aceteam-nodes[llm] instead of aceteam-nodes so aceteam-aep
  (our litellm replacement) gets pulled in automatically
- Update CLAUDE.md PR description guidelines to require Why/What/How
  structure in the Context section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix install script race: use Start-Process -Wait for GUI subsystem exe

With console=False (WINDOWS subsystem), PowerShell's & operator does
NOT wait for the process to complete — it fires and forgets. This
caused the daemon to start before --register finished, reading the
config with a stale registration_token instead of the new api_key.

Fix: use Start-Process -Wait -NoNewWindow for --register and --version
commands. This forces synchronous execution regardless of PE subsystem.

  selectors work in E2E navigation tests

Post 011: V9b results — offline data scaling hits 90.0% OOD CLIP

V9b (2132 prompts, 6000 steps offline FM) achieves exactly the log2 scaling
law prediction. All three metrics improved vs V7: CLIP 88.9%→90.0%,
aesthetic 5.905→5.939, LPIPS 0.668→0.664.

Confirmed offline FM strictly superior to online FM (V9a: +0.4pp CLIP but
−0.36 aesthetic; V9b: +1.1pp CLIP AND +0.03 aesthetic). Scaling law now
confirmed at 3 data points. Next: V9c (4000 prompts→91.3%) or rank-128.

then x-forwarded-host, then falls back to host header.

When deployed behind a reverse proxy with a path prefix (e.g. /hub),
API URLs in skill.md need to include the base path. Uses BASE_PATH
env var (e.g. BASE_PATH=/hub).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace code generator with Roslyn source generators (#30)

* Refactor MTG.Objects to use source generators

Replaced the legacy `MTG.Object.Generator` project with a new structure leveraging Roslyn source generators (`MtgEnumGenerator` and `MtgSetsGenerator`) for dynamic enum and set generation based on MTGJson data.

- Added `MTG.Objects.SourceGenerator` and `MTG.Objects.SourceGenerator.Tasks` projects.
- Introduced MSBuild tasks for downloading and caching JSON data files.
- Updated `Edition` class to use generated `Sets` for lookups.
- Removed legacy code, including services, models, and hosted tasks.
- Replaced `Newtonsoft.Json` with `System.Text.Json`.
- Added unit tests for source generators to ensure correctness.
- Improved build process with `.targets` integration and NuGet packaging.
- Enhanced code quality with modern C# features and better diagnostics.

* Modernize project and upgrade to .NET 10.0

- Updated CI/CD workflows to use .NET 10.0 and build `Tasks` project first.
- Removed `MTG.Objects.SourceGenerator.Tasks` project from the solution.
- Upgraded dependencies across projects, including `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis`, `System.Text.Json`, and test-related packages.
- Updated `MTG.Objects.csproj` to version `1.0.0.0` for a major release.
- Reintroduced `.editorconfig` with detailed coding conventions and removed redundant rules.
- Removed unused files and dependencies, including `JetBrains.Annotations` and `IsExternalInit`.
- Streamlined solution and project configurations for maintainability and alignment with modern standards.

* Upgrade GitHub Actions to latest versions

Updated GitHub Actions workflows to use the latest versions of actions for improved performance, compatibility, and maintainability:
- Replaced `actions/checkout` with `v6`.
- Replaced `actions/setup-dotnet` with `v5`.
- Replaced `actions/upload-artifact` with `v5`.
- Replaced `actions/download-artifact` with `v6`.
- Replaced `github/codeql-action` (`init`, `autobuild`, `analyze`) with `v4`.
- Replaced `actions/setup-java` with `v5` and updated `java-version` to `'11'` with `distribution: 'temurin'`.
- Replaced `actions/cache` with `v4`.

Added or updated parameters for better alignment with newer action versions:
- Added `source-url` for `actions/setup-dotnet`.
- Adjusted `retention-days` to `5` for `actions/upload-artifact` and ensured `if-no-files-found: error`.

Removed redundant or outdated actions to ensure consistency and reliability across workflows.

* Remove --no-restore flag from dotnet build command

* Improve logging and build verbosity in CI/CD pipeline

* Add detailed diagnostics to source generators

Introduce extensive diagnostic logging and error reporting to MtgEnumGenerator and MtgSetsGenerator. Emit diagnostics at key steps (initialization, file/attribute detection, JSON parsing, code generation) and report detailed errors with stack traces. Also, update CI build to use /v:diagnostic for improved traceability. These changes improve debugging and traceability of generator behavior in CI and IDE environments.

* Improve error handling and add retry for downloads

- Add Polly-based retry policy for HTTP downloads in DownloadMtgJsonDataTask, improving resilience to transient failures.
- Change task behavior to fail the build if downloads and cached files are both unavailable.
- Enhance logging for download retries and errors.
- In MtgSetsGenerator, fail the build with diagnostics if SetList.json is missing or empty.
- Update .editorconfig to enforce final newlines and suppress RS2008.
- Add Polly NuGet package reference.

* Adjust build verbosity for Tasks and main build steps

Removed /v:diagnostic from the Tasks project build to reduce output verbosity, and added it to the main build step to provide more detailed build logs. This change helps focus diagnostic output on the main build process.

* Improve fallback logic for cached MTGJson data files

Updated error handling to require both "EnumValues.json" and "SetList.json" in the cache before falling back after a download failure. Also made minor formatting changes to variable declarations in EnsureDataFileAsync.

* fix: repair CI/CD and CodeQL pipeline failures

- Remove unnecessary .targets import from SourceGenerator.csproj that
  caused DownloadMtgJsonDataTask to run during generator build (MSB4181)
- Update CI/CD workflow to install .NET 10.0.x SDK (needed for net10.0
  test projects) alongside 6.0.x
- Replace CodeQL autobuild with explicit build steps that build the
  Tasks project first, ensuring source-generated types are available
- Add Log.LogError() before returning false in DownloadMtgJsonDataTask
  to comply with MSBuild task conventions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove Polly dependency from MSBuild task

Polly DLL couldn't be resolved at runtime by MSBuild, causing download
task failures on CI (fresh checkout with no cached files). Replace with
a simple manual retry loop since only exponential backoff is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use Release config and --no-build for test step

Test step was rebuilding in Debug mode (default) where the Tasks DLL
didn't exist, causing source generator data files to be missing. Use
-c Release --no-build to reuse the already-built Release binaries.
Also remove /v:diagnostic from build step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove obsolete Generator Runner and fix remaining workflows

- Delete MTG.Objects.Generator-Runner.yml — the old code generation
  approach has been replaced by Roslyn source generators at build time
- Add .NET 6.0.x SDK to linux-mac and windows workflows (needed for
  net6.0 test projects alongside net10.0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add SourceGenerator.Tasks project to solution

The Tasks project was missing from the solution file, causing
dotnet restore at solution level to skip it. This made --no-restore
fail in workflows that build the Tasks project separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: address SonarCloud code quality findings

- Extract MtgEnumGenerator.GenerateEnums into smaller methods to reduce
  cognitive complexity from 32 to within the 15 allowed
- Extract XML doc comment generation into AppendDocComment helper to
  eliminate repeated string literals in MtgSetsGenerator
- Remove explicit nameof() args from Guard.Against calls in Edition and
  NumberOfCards — modern GuardClauses uses CallerArgumentExpression

- Extract shared exception reporting into DiagnosticHelper to eliminate
  duplicated catch blocks between MtgEnumGenerator and MtgSetsGenerator

- Add regex timeout to Deck.cs to prevent ReDoS (S6444)
- Add USER vscode to devcontainer Dockerfile to avoid running as root (S6471)
- Move secrets from inline run expansion to env vars in Windows workflow (S7636)
- Migrate from deprecated sonar.login to sonar.token parameter

* fix: add regex timeout to source generators (S6444)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.22.58: Sentinel page PAGE_READWRITE — eliminate write-skip VEH flood

Changed sentinel page from PAGE_READONLY to PAGE_READWRITE. The write-skip
VEH flood (~33K/sec at +0x2D67C8) was consuming 100% CPU on New Game —
same whack-a-mole problem as the read floods.

With PAGE_READWRITE, engine writes to sentinel succeed silently — zero VEH
overhead. Vtable/formFlags corruption is handled by watchdog repair (every
10s tick) + existing CASE 0/EXT-CALL-RET handlers for interim crashes.

Combined with patches C-F (read floods), this should eliminate ALL major
VEH overhead during New Game initialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
